description: We build a cloud‑based service that automatically calculates, collects and remits sales tax for online merchants. Our platform plugs into most e‑commerce carts and promises to keep businesses compliant with minimal effort.
target_customer: Small‑to‑medium e‑commerce brands that sell across multiple US states and need a simple way to handle sales tax without hiring a specialist.
pricing: Subscription‑based, starting at $49/month for up to $5K monthly sales volume, with higher tiers for larger merchants (e.g., $199/month for up to $50K).
traction: We have a handful of beta users and a waiting list of a few dozen sign‑ups, but no paying customers yet and no concrete revenue metrics.
team: Founder John Doe, former software engineer at a large marketplace platform; co‑founder Maya Patel, previously a consultant on tax compliance for e‑commerce clients.
problem: Online sellers struggle to keep up with constantly changing sales‑tax rules across states, leading to compliance risk and manual overhead.
solution: Our API pulls the latest tax rates, applies them to each transaction in real time, and can generate filing reports for each jurisdiction, reducing the need for manual calculations.

name: HealCollect
description: HealCollect is a cloud‑native platform that modernises health‑care debt collection with AI‑driven outreach, real‑time compliance monitoring and integrated payment options. We aim to cut manual workflow, improve patient experience and raise recovery rates for providers.
target_customer: Hospital finance departments and large physician groups that own the receivables; they pay because faster, compliant collections reduce write‑offs and avoid costly attorney fees.
pricing: A SaaS subscription from $2,500 /mo for up to $150k in monthly receivables plus a 1.2% success‑fee on recovered balances (capped at $5k per month). Custom enterprise pricing available for larger networks.
traction: Beta launched Q1 2025 with a regional hospital system (2 M in outstanding balances) – we’ve recovered $180k in the first 8 weeks and reduced manual effort by 40%. Early conversations with three additional health‑systems are in the pipeline.
team: Founder & CEO Maya Patel spent 6 years at a big‑four advisory practice building payment‑technology solutions for insurers; CTO Jason Liu is a former software lead at a health‑tech startup and currently works part‑time while we raise seed capital.
problem: Healthcare providers still rely on legacy, paper‑based collection processes that are slow, error‑prone and often run afoul of HIPAA and state debt‑collection regulations, leading to high write‑offs and patient dissatisfaction.
solution: HealCollect automates the entire outreach cycle – from AI‑segmented patient communication (email, SMS, voice) to secure online payment portals and real‑time compliance checks – giving providers a single dashboard to track, manage and recover outstanding balances efficiently.

name: GoalReward
description: GoalReward lets consumers link their personal savings goals to a loyalty‑points ecosystem. Every time a user hits a milestone—like saving $100 or staying on track for a month—they earn redeemable points from partner merchants, turning boring budgeting into a game.
target_customer: We sell the platform to banks and neobanks that want to boost user stickiness, and to retailers looking for a new acquisition channel. End‑users get the benefit for free, but the financial institutions and merchants pay for the integration.
pricing: We’re still solidifying the model, but the idea is a low‑base monthly fee per active user (roughly $0.50‑$1.00) plus a small transaction‑share on redeemed points. We also plan a revenue‑share tier for high‑volume partners, though the exact percentages are TBD.
traction: In the past six months we’ve run a closed beta with two regional banks and a boutique retailer, driving a 12% lift in weekly savings deposits among 1,200 test users. Our API has processed $45k in virtual points, and both partners have expressed interest in a full rollout.
team: I’m Maya Patel, former product lead at a top‑10 US bank where I built a rewards program that grew deposits by 8%. My co‑founder, Luis Ortega, is a seasoned engineer from a fintech startup that exited to a major payment processor.
problem: People know they should save, but traditional savings accounts offer no immediate gratification, so motivation wanes and deposits stay flat.
solution: GoalReward injects instant, tangible rewards into the savings journey by converting progress into points that can be spent at everyday merchants, creating a feedback loop that keeps users engaged and nudges them toward their financial goals.

name: HarvestPay
description: HarvestPay is a white‑label API that gives niche online farmer‑market platforms the ability to push funds to their sellers the instant they make a sale. We handle the banking integration, compliance and risk‑engine so the marketplace can focus on users, not payouts.
target_customer: We sell to the marketplace operators (e.g., Farm2Table, GreenPatch) who are willing to pay for a better seller experience because it drives higher retention and transaction volume.
pricing: We charge a flat $0.25 per payout plus 0.5% of the transferred amount; for marketplaces under $100k monthly volume we offer a $199/month starter tier with a bulk discount after $50k volume.
traction: Beta launched with Farm2Table in March – 120 of their 150 sellers have opted in, and average payout speed has gone from 3‑5 business days to under 2 minutes. The marketplace reports a 12% lift in weekly active sellers since integration.
team: Founder Alex Liu spent 5 years building payments infrastructure at Stripe and 2 years running the finance team at a regional food‑hub marketplace.
problem: Small‑scale marketplace sellers (artisan growers, boutique producers) often wait days for cash, which hurts inventory replenishment and makes them vulnerable to larger platforms that promise instant payouts.
solution: HarvestPay plugs directly into the marketplace’s checkout flow, instantly settles the seller’s bank account via our partner bank network, and provides real‑time compliance checks, so sellers get paid the second a buyer’s card is authorized.
